topic: Can the system-spec-kit memory MCP (mk-spec-memory, 37 tools) be replaced by a CLI with ZERO feature loss? Evaluate three architectures against the zero-feature-loss bar across 5 key questions (parity matrix, daemon-dependency audit, MCP-only affordances, integration-surface migration, architecture comparison with go/no-go).

noveltyJustification: First broad pass that enumerates every tool from TOOL_NAMES sets and cross-references the existing CLI prior art; the 37-row matrix is net-new and reduces the search space substantially.

ruledOut.approach: 11 CLIs framing as broad parity evidence
ruledOut.reason: 10/11 are operational utilities (ast-parser, backfill-frontmatter, cleanup-orphaned-vectors, rank-memories, rebuild-auto-entities, etc.), not user-facing tool substitutes

ruledOut.approach: Assume KQ1 = KQ1.1 only; KQ2/KQ3 separate
ruledOut.reason: KQ1's matrix completeness is necessary but not sufficient for zero-feature-loss; protocol affordances in KQ3 must be evaluated against the same matrix

noveltyJustification: First iteration to map concrete daemon-resident services (hf-model-server sidecar, chokidar watcher, job queue, RSS watchdog, single-writer lease, working-memory cache) to specific tool handlers and produce the per-architecture loss table — the search space shrinks materially.

ruledOut.approach: Treat S6 warm session briefs as a daemon-only cache
ruledOut.reason: working-memory.ts is per-session SQLite rows; the 'warm' is just the open DB connection in the calling process — works in all three architectures

ruledOut.approach: Assume single-writer lease requires the daemon
ruledOut.reason: spec-folder-mutex.ts:14-86 is interprocess (mkdir-based) by design; works across processes for all three architectures

focus: KQ3 — MCP-only affordances and concrete CLI replacements
noveltyJustification: First iteration to map each MCP-only affordance to a concrete, file:line-anchored replacement. Replacements vary by runtime (Claude / OpenCode / Codex / Copilot); the answer is 'replaced with N different mechanisms per runtime', not 'lost'. 5x3 master map + 4x5 cross-runtime matrix reduce the search space substantially.

ruledOut.approach: Treat A1 tool-schema auto-discovery as a uniform problem
ruledOut.reason: Per-runtime problem; OpenCode case is the only one that needs runtime support (the 'tools:' block); Claude/Codex/Copilot use existing permission surfaces

ruledOut.approach: Treat A5 auto-surface as a single replacement problem
ruledOut.reason: Architecture-dependent: (a) loses it; (b)/(c) keep it. Migration cost for (a) is documentation + agent-prompt updates

noveltyJustification: Per-file grep -c counts turn 'many references' into a concrete effort estimate; 5 surfaces catalogued (S1..S5); 28 files / ~125 references; S3 runtime config is the only external dependency (1-3 week gate).

ruledOut.reason: Inline tool calls in prompt bodies (e.g. mcp__mk_spec_memory__memory_search({...})) require rewriting to CLI flag syntax; cost is ~2x a pure search-and-replace
ruledOut.evidence: iteration-004.md finding 4.8 (34 refs in manage.md are all inline calls)

ruledOut.reason: OpenCode 'tools:' block is an external runtime dependency; either OpenCode ships it (1-3 weeks) or we ship a CLI shim (2-3 days). S3 is the migration critical path.

noveltyJustification: Synthesis iteration: combines KQ1..KQ4 into a single answer. Per-architecture scoring matrix (5 dimensions), risk register (9 risks), effort estimate (1 engineer, 12-16 days), and final go/no-go. Verdict: GO with architecture (c) — hybrid CLI that auto-spawns the daemon on demand.

ruledOut.reason: Daemon is multi-process by design (Node parent + hf-model-server sidecar + SQLite daemon); CLI is one entry point, not one process
Migration is composable per-tool and per-runtime; the S3 runtime config gate is the only true atomic step
ruledOut.evidence: iteration-005.md finding 5.8; 28 files / 125 refs split across 4 mechanical surfaces + 1 runtime gate
